data.title: Xilio Therapeutics announces 1-for-14 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Xilio Therapeutics (XLO) on Thursday said it will implement a 1-for-14 reverse stock split of the issued shares of the company’s common stock, effective March 13, 2026.
  • One of the primary goals of the reverse stock split is to increase the per-share market price of the company’s common stock to enable the company to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on Nasdaq.
  • The company’s common stock is expected to begin trading on a split-adjusted basis when the markets open on March 16, 2026 under the company’s existing ticker “XLO”.
  • The reverse stock will reduce the number of outstanding shares of Xilio’s common stock from about 73.5 million shares to about 5.2 million shares.
  • XLO -16.48% premarket to $0.451.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Halozyme appoints David Ramsay as interim CFO
data.description:
• Halozyme Therapeutics (HALO) announced on Thursday that David Ramsay has been appointed interim chief financial officer ((CFO)), effective March 23, 2026.
  • The company said Ramsay will oversee all financial operations while the company continues its active search for a permanent CFO.
  • Ramsay previously served as Halozyme's chief financial officer from 2003 to 2009 and again from 2013 to 2015, during which time the company evolved from a private enterprise to a billion‑dollar public biopharmaceutical company.
  • The company said its executive search for a permanent CFO is well underway, and it has engaged a leading executive search firm to identify candidates who bring the combination of capital markets expertise and strategy.
  • HALO -0.88% premarket to $65.89.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bumble's Q4 results give investors hope that 'heavy lifting' is done
data.description:
Shares of Bumble (BMBL) are set to open on Thursday at their highest level since January, as the company’s fourth quarter results were not as bad as analysts feared and efforts to innovate the dating app with AI functionality are yielding measurable results.

“We executed according to plan in the fourth quarter, delivering results at the high end of our guidance ranges while completing the most intensive phase of our member base reset,” said Kevin Cook, Bumble CFO.

The company recently added a series of AI-powered features to help users select more flattering photos of themselves and give feedback on profile bios as part of an effort to help distinguish Bumble (BMBL) from competitors Hinge and Match (MTCH).  

Management noted that with the “heavy lifting” of its reset behind it, Bumble is accelerating product innovation and prioritizing member experience enhancements.

"We are building from a stronger base and positioning Bumble for its next chapter of product-led growth,” said Bumble Founder and CEO Whitney Wolfe Herd.

For the final quarter of the year, although Bumble (BMBL) reported a loss of $4.06 per share with revenue down 14.3% to $224.2M, average revenue per paying user increased 8% during the quarter and was up 2% for the year.  

For the current quarter, Bumble (BMBL) sees revenue within the range of $209M and $213M, which straddles the consensus estimate of $210.7M, while adjusted EBITDA is seen between $76M and $80M, an increase of 21% year-over-year at the midpoint.

The results and upbeat outlook earned an upgrade to Neutral from Underweight at J.P. Morgan on Thursday with analyst Cory Carpenter acknowledging the results were better than anticipated, noting that a 159K drop in app net additions was much better than the -275K that was expected.

“Bumble worked through its shrink to grow phase quicker than we expected, with the focus now shifting to product innovation,” Carpenter said.

While Bumble (BMBL) still has a “long road ahead to get back to sustainable revenue growth,” J.P. Morgan’s Carpenter no longer thinks an Underweight rating is appropriate “with leading indicators stabilizing, a potential catalyst in the Bumble 2.0 launch in the second quarter, and shares trading at 2.5x EBITDA vs 7.5x for competitor Match.”

Shares are more than 20% higher in Thursday's premarket trading.

data.title: Karman Space & Defense appoints Jon Rambeau as CEO
data.description:
•  Karman Space & Defense (KRMN) announced on Thursday a transition in leadership with chief executive officer (CEO) Tony Koblinski retiring after a 40-year career in leadership and Jon Rambeau assuming the CEO position effective March 23, 2026.
  • Most recently,  Rambeau worked as president of the $8 billion Communications & Spectrum Dominance segment for L3Harris Technologies. 
  • Before his time with L3Harris, Rambeau spent more than 25 years at Lockheed Martin, where he held senior executive roles across a broad spectrum of defense capabilities. 
  • KRMN -1.59% premarket to $103.17.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ESAB announces offering of $1B senior notes
data.description:
• ESAB (ESAB) said on Thursday that it intends to offer $1B of senior notes due 2031.
  • The company said it intends to use net proceeds from the notes sale to fund part of the purchase price for the acquisition of Eddyfi Holding and related entities.
  • The notes will be guaranteed by certain of its current and future domestic restricted subsidiaries.

data.title: Canada posts C$3.65B trade deficit in January
Justera appoints Andrew Ryu as interim CEO
data.description:
• Justera Health (SCRSF) has appointed Andrew Ryu as its interim chief executive officer and chairman of the board while the company advances its operational and corporate initiatives.
  • Ryu is a graduate of McMaster University and the University of Toronto. He was the founder and CEO of Loyalist Group (LOY), which was recognized as a top TSXV Company in 2013 and ranked fourth and named pick of the street in 2014. He later served as CEO and chairman of Datametrex (DM) until May 2022.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: JFB pushes 2-for-1 stock split date before XTEND AI Robotics listing
data.description:
• JFB Construction (JFB) updated the timing of its planned 2-for-1 stock split of issued and outstanding common shares from March 20 to March 24 as part of its $1.5B all-stock merger with XTEND.
  • This split will be carried out for shareholders of record as of March 23, 2026, with investors receiving one additional share for every share held.
  • Trading of the common stock is expected to begin on a split-adjusted basis after market close on March 23 on Nasdaq under the ticker JFB.
  • After the stock split, the outstanding shares will increase to about 14.0M from roughly 7.0M, with no change to the stock’s par value.
  • Joseph F. Basile III, CEO of JFB, said the stock split aims to lower the per-share price and increase the outstanding shares to broaden investor access ahead of the planned XTEND AI Robotics (XTND) NASDAQ listing.
  • JFB Construction Holdings (JFB) shares traded lower to around $17.54 in premarket hours.

data.title: Oddity Tech approves $200M stock buyback program
data.description:
• Oddity Tech (ODD) announced on Thursday that it has approved a share buyback program authorizing the repurchase of up to $200M of its Class A ordinary shares.
  • Under the new plan, the company will replace its earlier $150M buyback program, with the authorization set to expire on March 31, 2029, or when funds are fully used.
  • The company had repurchased about $97M under its prior buyback authorization, including about $50M year-to-date.
  • Shares +3.63%.

data.title: Oracle to increase restructuring costs by $500M; Citi reassured by results, guidance
data.description:
• In conjunction with its strong quarterly results and outlook, Oracle (ORCL) disclosed that it will spend $2.1B on restructuring costs in fiscal 2026, $500M more than previously stated.
  • The IT giant said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission that the total estimated restructuring costs will be $2.1B. Additionally, it has already recorded $156M and $982M of restructuring costs in connection with the plan for the three and nine months ended Feb. 28, respectively. In December, Oracle estimated its total restructuring costs would be $1.6B. 
  • Oracle is implementing the layoffs, which are impacting thousands of employees, due in part to its immense spending on expanding data center capacity for artificial intelligence.
  • Separately, Citi analysts said on Thursday that the quarterly results were “reassuring” and helped “refute ongoing concerns around financing/data center delays.” As such, the firm reiterated its Buy rating on Oracle and raised its price target to $320 from $310.
